topic: A person discovers their child has the same teacher they had
raw_output:
[story 1]

The crisp autumn air did little to cool the knot tightening in my stomach. I stared at the enrollment form, the ink mocking me with its neat, familiar script. Sarah. My daughter, Lily, was in the same fourth-grade class as the child who had been my own teacher, Mrs. Albright. A slow, cold wave washed over me, making my hands tremble slightly as I gripped the edge of the desk. It wasn't just a coincidence; it felt like a deliberate, sickening arrangement. I reread the teacher’s name, the memory of her cheerful, overly saccharine demeanor suddenly tainted with a profound sense of wrongness. I pushed the papers away, the sound sharp in the quiet room.

[story 2]

"Are you sure about this, Mark?" my partner asked, noticing the rigidity in my posture. I didn't look up from the school directory. The revelation hung heavy in the air, suffocating. Lily’s class roster, the name staring back at me—Mrs. Albright. A tight clench formed in my jaw. I felt a sudden, intense need to scrub my hands on my jeans, a purely physical reaction to the sheer unpleasantness of the situation. Every word Mrs. Albright had ever spoken echoed in my mind, warped now, stripped bare of any warmth, leaving only a hollow, unsettling residue.

[story 3]

I traced the cursive handwriting on the permission slip with a fingertip, the texture of the paper rough beneath my touch. It was impossible. My child sharing a classroom with the woman who had shaped my own early years. A deep, visceral churning started low in my abdomen, a heavy pressure that made breathing shallow. I stood up abruptly, pacing the length of the living room, my movements jerky and agitated. I kept catching glimpses of Mrs. Albright’s smiling face in my periphery, and a sourness rose in my throat, thick and unwelcome. I needed to leave, needed distance from that specific constellation of shared history.
